# This is the CMakeCache file.
# For build in directory: /home/wangmin/sel4-test/temp/11.0.0/build
# It was generated by CMake: /usr/local/bin/cmake
# You can edit this file to change values found and used by cmake.
# If you do not want to change any of the values, simply exit the editor.
# If you do want to change a value, simply edit, save, and exit the editor.
# The syntax for the file is as follows:
# KEY:TYPE=VALUE
# KEY is the name of a variable in the cache.
# TYPE is a hint to GUIs for the type of VALUE, DO NOT EDIT TYPE!.
# VALUE is the current value for the KEY.

########################
# EXTERNAL cache entries
########################

KERNEL_PATH:STRING=/home/wangmin/sel4-test/temp/11.0.0/kernel

//Architecture to use when building the kernel
KernelArch:STRING=riscv

//Enable benchamrks including logging and tracing info.     Setting
// this value > 1 enables a 1MB log buffer and functions for extracting
// data from it     at user level. NOTE this is only tested on
// the sabre and will not work on platforms with < 512mb memory.
//     This is not fully implemented for x86.     none -> No Benchmarking
// features enabled.     generic -> Enable global benchmarks config
// variable with no specific features.     track_kernel_entries
// -> Log kernel entries information including timing, number of
// invocations and arguments for     system calls, interrupts,
// user faults and VM faults.     tracepoints -> Enable manually
// inserted tracepoints that the kernel will track time consumed
// between.     track_utilisation -> Enable the kernel to track
// each thread's utilisation time.
KernelBenchmarks:STRING=none

//In debug mode, seL4 prints diagnostic messages to its serial
// output describing,     e.g., the cause of system call errors.
// This setting determines whether ANSI escape     codes are applied
// to colour code these error messages. You may wish to disable
// this     setting if your serial output is redirected to a file
// or pipe.
KernelColourPrinting:BOOL=ON

//Adds a system call that allows users to specify code to be run
// in kernel mode.     Useful for profiling.
KernelDangerousCodeInjection:BOOL=OFF

//Enable debug facilities (symbols and assertions) in the kernel
KernelDebugBuild:BOOL=ON

//Enable -fwhole-program when linking kernel. This should work
// modulo gcc bugs, which     are not uncommon with -fwhole-program.
// Consider this feature experimental!
KernelFWholeProgram:BOOL=OFF

//Enable IPC fastpath
KernelFastpath:BOOL=ON

//seL4 does not properly check for and handle spurious interrupts.
// This can result     in unnecessary output from the kernel during
// debug builds. If you are CERTAIN these     messages are benign
// then use this config to turn them off.
KernelIRQReporting:BOOL=ON

//Use the MCS kernel configuration, which is not verified.
KernelIsMCS:BOOL=OFF

//Max number of bootinfo untyped caps
KernelMaxNumBootinfoUntypedCaps:STRING=230

KernelMaxNumNodes:STRING=1

//Maximum number of work units (delete/revoke iterations) until
// the kernel checks for    pending interrupts (and preempts the
// currently running syscall if interrupts are pending).
KernelMaxNumWorkUnitsPerPreemption:STRING=100

KernelNumDomains:STRING=1

//The number of priority levels per domain. Valid range 1-256
KernelNumPriorities:STRING=256

//Select the kernel optimisation level
KernelOptimisation:STRING=-O2

//Number of page     table levels for RISC-V depends on the mode.
// For example there are:     2, 3 and 4 levels on Sv32, Sv39,
// Sv48 RISC-V paging modes respectively.
KernelPTLevels:STRING=3

KernelPlatform:STRING=spike

//Allow the kernel to print out messages to the serial console
// during bootup and execution.
KernelPrinting:BOOL=ON

//Maximum size in bits of chunks of memory to zero before checking
// a preemption point.
KernelResetChunkBits:STRING=8

//Maximum number of objects that can be created in a single Retype()
// invocation.
KernelRetypeFanOutLimit:STRING=256

KernelSel4Arch:STRING=riscv64

//This describes the log2 size of the kernel stack. Great care
// should be taken as    there is no guard below the stack so setting
// this too small will cause random    memory corruption
KernelStackBits:STRING=12

//Number of timer ticks until a thread is preempted.
KernelTimeSlice:STRING=5

//Timer tick period in milliseconds
KernelTimerTickMS:STRING=2

//On a double fault the kernel can try and print out the users
// stack to aid     debugging. This option determines how many
// words of stack should be printed.
KernelUserStackTraceLength:STRING=16

//When enabled this configuration option prevents the usage of
// any other options that    would compromise the verification
// story of the kernel. Enabling this option does NOT    imply
// you are using a verified kernel.
KernelVerificationBuild:BOOL=OFF

//This flag causes CMake to add lots of source files to its dependency
// lists
//\n     which slows down its configuration times.
//\n     When not making changes in the musllibc directory, it is
// likely not necessary
//\n     to have this enabled.
LIB_MUSLC_GLOB_SOURCES:BOOL=OFF

//Use `make -j` when building libmuslc
LIB_MUSLC_PARALLEL_BUILD:BOOL=true

LWIP_HELPERS:STRING=/home/wangmin/sel4-test/temp/11.0.0/projects/util_libs/liblwip/lwip_helpers.cmake

//Number of preallocated DMA buffers
//\n    To avoid allocating and freeing buffers continuously the
// driver
//\n    can preallocate a base amount internally.
LibEthdriverNumPreallocatedBuffers:STRING=512

//Async driver for PicoTcp
//\n    Use an async instead of a polling driver for PicoTCP.
LibEthdriverPicoTCBAsyncDriver:BOOL=ON

//Size of preallocated DMA buffers
//\n    The size of each preallocated buffer that will be used for
// RX and
//\n    TX allocation requests. This needs to be the maximum of
// the RX buffer
//\n    size and the MTU. Currently the largest RX buffer of any
// of the
//\n    implemented drivers is 2048, and the MTU is 1500
LibEthdriverPreallocatedBufSize:STRING=2048

//Number of RX descriptors in the descriptor ring for the driver.
LibEthdriverRXDescCount:STRING=128

//Number of TX descriptors in the descriptor ring for the driver.
LibEthdriverTXDescCount:STRING=128

//Build LwIP
LibLwip:BOOL=OFF

//Build Nanopb and related libraries
LibNanopb:BOOL=ON

//Build libpicotcp
LibPicotcp:BOOL=OFF

//Allow the VMM to install arbitrary devices into the VM as they
// are accessed.
LibSel4ArmVMMOnDemandDeviceInstall:BOOL=ON

//Support vchan connections
LibSel4ArmVMMVchanSupport:BOOL=OFF

//Pointer tracking buffer entries     Size of buffer to use for
// tracking memory allocations within     instrumentation. This
// setting has no effect if you are not using the     allocation
// instrumentation. Setting this value to 0 disables pointer  
//   tracking.
LibSel4DebugAllocBufferEntries:STRING=0

//Function instrumentation to apply to function calls. This setting
// will only have     an effect when your CFLAGS include -finstrument-functions,
// in which     case __cyg_profile_func_enter will be called on
// every function entry     and __cyg_profile_func_exit will be
// called on every function exit. The     value of this setting
// determines the implementation of     __cyg_profile_func_enter
// and __cyg_profile_func_exit supplied by this     library. Regardless
// of the option you select, you can always override     the implementation
// by providing your own in your application.     none ->     
//    No implementation of __cyg_profile_func_enter and       
//  __cyg_profile_func_exit are provided.     printf ->       
//  Print entry and exit of every function call. This option is
// useful for         a very coarse and noisy description of what
// your code is doing.     backtrace ->         Track function
// calls for the purposes of a backtrace. You will need to    
//     enable this option if you want to retrieve programmatic
// backtraces.
LibSel4DebugFunctionInstrumentation:STRING=none

//Function attributes     default->Verification friendly default
// configuration. syscalls will be inlined,         but generated
// functions will not.     inline->When set to true will mark generated
// functions as 'inline', allowing         them to be inlined by
// the callee user code. This may be undesirable         for verification,
// so setting to 'n' will forcibly prevent the function       
//  from being inlined.     public->When set to true will make
// all user facing functions available as         public symbols,
// which can be convenient for some language bindings.
LibSel4FunctionAttributes:STRING=inline

//Make __arch_putchar a weak symbol     This allows you to override
// __arch_putchar in another library or your     application.
LibSel4MuslcSysArchPutcharWeak:BOOL=OFF

//Implementation of a simple file system usi g CPIO archives  
//   If this is enabled open and read syscalls will attempt to
// use the cpio archive     _cpio_archive. This implements a basic
// read only POSIX interface to that file system
LibSel4MuslcSysCPIOFS:BOOL=OFF

//Perform seL4_DebugHalt on _exit and _abort     Controls whether
// or not a DebugHalt should be called to implement Exit     style
// syscalls. This is useful if you have a single app and thread,
// but     should be turned off if using multiple threads that
// may wish to exit     independently of the whole system
LibSel4MuslcSysDebugHalt:BOOL=ON

//Malloc limit     This value sets the number of bytes reserved
// for the region malloc     allocates from. Note that you won't
// actually be able to allocate this     entire amount because
// there is some bookkeeping overhead. This area is     allocated
// statically.
LibSel4MuslcSysMorecoreBytes:STRING=1048576

//Supply _start entry point. Emit _start with a default definition
// that sets up a     stack and does what's required to enter main().
LibSel4PlatSupportStart:BOOL=OFF

//Redirect putchar() to seL4_DebugPutchar()
LibSel4PlatSupportUseDebugPutChar:BOOL=ON

//Annotate output with different ansi colours for each client
LibSel4SerialServerColoredOutput:BOOL=ON

//use only IPC buffer for syscalls. When generating syscall wrappers,
// only use the     IPC buffer for marshalling and unmarshalling
// arguments. Without this option set,     arguments will be passed
// in registers where possible for better performance.
LibSel4StubsUseIPCBufferOnly:BOOL=OFF

//Supply _sel4_start entry point. Emit a _sel4_start that sets
// up a stack and does     what's required to enter a regular _start
// routine
LibSel4SupportSel4Start:BOOL=ON

//Print XML output. This will     buffer the test output and print
// in <stdout> blocks so that bamboo     can parse the test logs
// properly. Turn this off for debugging your test suite
LibSel4TestPrintXML:BOOL=OFF

//Halt on the first test failure     Default behaviour is to try
// and run the test suite, regardless of any failures.     Turning
// this on will stop the test suite on the first error
LibSel4TestPrinterHaltOnTestFailure:BOOL=OFF

//A POSIX regex pattern used to filter tests
LibSel4TestPrinterRegex:STRING=.*

//Size of default cspace to spawn processes with
LibSel4UtilsCSpaceSizeBits:STRING=12

//Profiling tools     Enables the functionality of a set of profiling
// tools. When disabled these profiling tools     will compile
// down to nothing.
LibSel4UtilsProfile:BOOL=OFF

//Size of stacks in bytes to allocate if using vspace interface
// in this library
LibSel4UtilsStackSize:STRING=65536

//Enable VMM debug output
LibSel4VMMDebug:BOOL=ON

//VMM Debug Output
//\n    Debug verbosity level.
//\n    5 levels for debug messages:
//\n       0: Always printout
//\n       1: Main entry point in a module
//\n       2: 2nd level entry point in a module
//\n       3: Main entry point of a function
//\n       4: Details inside a function
LibSel4VMMDebugLevel:STRING=3

//Ignore EPT Violations
//\n    If set then EPT faults will be ignored and the guest will
// be resumed
LibSel4VMMIgnoreEPTViolation:BOOL=OFF

//Expose VESA Frame Buffer
//\n    Support for mapping the VESA frame buffer frames into the
// guest OS.
//\n    This option is useful for working around non-working video
// drivers otherwise,
//\n    due to missing features or such.
LibSel4VMMVESAFrameBuffer:BOOL=ON

//Use VMX Pre-Emption timer for debugging
//\n    Will cause a regular vmexit to happen based on VMX pre-emption
//\n    timer. At each exit the guest state will be printed out.
// This
//\n    can be used to aid debugging when running a guest causes
// nothing
//\n    to appear to happen
LibSel4VMMVMXTimerDebug:BOOL=OFF

//Default ZF_LOG_LEVEL for the ZF_LOG library. 5 is LOG_LEVEL_ERROR.
// 0 is     the most verbose setting.
LibUtilsDefaultZfLogLevel:STRING=5

//Leak memory on free     Changes the default free functions to
// silently leak memory     instead of assert failing.     This
// is useful in scenarios where code is correctly freeing     resources,
// but you want to use an allocator that does not     implement
// free.
LibVKAAllowMemoryLeaks:BOOL=OFF

//(debug allocator) live object buffer size     As for the above
// option, but sets the maximum number of objects that     can
// be tracked at any one time.
LibVKADebugLiveObjsSZ:STRING=0

//(debug allocator) live slot buffer size     VKA contains an allocator,
// debugvka, that wraps a target VKA allocator     for the purposes
// of debugging. It tracks slot and object allocations     and
// catches both caller problems like double free, and allocator
//     problems like handing out the same resource twice. This
// configuration     option sets the maximum number of CSlots that
// can be tracked at any one     time.
LibVKADebugLiveSlotsSZ:STRING=0

//Page size to be used for linker
LinkPageSize:STRING=0x1000

//MCS kernel
MCS:BOOL=OFF

//PL0 access to generic timer CNTPCT and CNTFRQ.     Grant user
// access to physical counter and counter     frequency registers
// of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens
// a timing     channel
KernelArmExportPCNTUser:INTERNAL=OFF
KernelArmExportPCNTUser_DISABLED:INTERNAL=TRUE
//PL0 access to generic timer CNTP_CTL and CNTP_CVAL.     Grant
// user access to physical timer registers of the generic timer.
//     WARNING: selecting this option opens a storage channel and
// allows threads to easily     corrupt these registers for each
// other, this should only be used for     debugging / development
// purposes
KernelArmExportPTMRUser:INTERNAL=OFF
KernelArmExportPTMRUser_DISABLED:INTERNAL=TRUE
//PL0 access to generic timer CNTVCT and CNTFRQ.     Grant user
// access to virtual counter and counter     frequency registers
// of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens
// a timing     channel
KernelArmExportVCNTUser:INTERNAL=OFF
KernelArmExportVCNTUser_DISABLED:INTERNAL=TRUE
//PL0 access to generic timer CNTV_CTL and CNTV_CVAL.     Grant
// user access to virtual timer registers of the generic timer.
//     WARNING: selecting this option opens a storage channel and
// allows threads to easily     corrupt these registers for each
// other, this should only be used for     debugging / development
// purposes
KernelArmExportVTMRUser:INTERNAL=OFF
KernelArmExportVTMRUser_DISABLED:INTERNAL=TRUE
